rationale_for_baseline_tier: Sudden severe unilateral pelvic pain with recurrent vomiting during exertion in a reproductive-age woman is a time-critical surgical abdomen — suspected ovarian torsion until proven otherwise, with ectopic pregnancy also on the table. Definitive care (transvaginal ultrasound, gynaecological surgery) is available only in hospital and the ovary is salvageable only if detorsion happens promptly, so emergency-department assessment today (T4) is the minimum correct action. It is escalated to T5 (call 000 ambulance) when the patient cannot be transported promptly and safely — which applies here given she is immobilised by pain and vomiting repeatedly, and the source clinician did dispatch an ambulance. This is not a peri-arrest, minute-to-minute life threat in the way anaphylaxis or cardiac arrest is, so T4 is the honest disease-acuity baseline with a low threshold to T5 for transport and for any sign of shock or rupture.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Severe unrelenting pelvic pain with persistent vomiting and inability to mobilise or be transported safely
escalation_edges.time_bound_condition: Now — call 000 for ambulance transport if she cannot get to an emergency department promptly and safely
escalation_edges.rationale: A suspected torsion is time-critical for ovarian salvage; a patient too unwell to self-transport needs ambulance transfer to a hospital with gynaecological surgery.
escalation_edges.communication_template: Because the pain is this severe and you keep vomiting and can't move, the safest thing is to call 000 now for an ambulance to take you straight to a hospital that can do a pelvic ultrasound and surgery if needed. Don't eat or drink anything.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Any possibility of pregnancy, positive pregnancy test, or shoulder-tip pain / faintness (possible ruptured ectopic)
escalation_edges.time_bound_condition: Immediately if faint, collapsing, or a positive pregnancy test with worsening pain
escalation_edges.rationale: A ruptured ectopic causes life-threatening intra-abdominal bleeding and needs an emergency ambulance, not self-transport.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If there's any chance you could be pregnant and you feel faint, dizzy, or the pain is getting worse, call 000 straight away — this can be an emergency that needs immediate treatment.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Sudden complete relief of the pain followed by fever, chills, or a swollen, distended, rigid abdomen
escalation_edges.rationale: A painless interval after severe torsion pain can mean the ovary has infarcted; subsequent fever and distension suggest necrosis and peritonitis requiring emergency care.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If the pain suddenly disappears completely and then you get a fever or your belly becomes swollen and hard, call 000 — that can be a sign the situation has become more serious.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Signs of shock — fainting, cold clammy skin, confusion, or a racing heart with lightheadedness
escalation_edges.rationale: Haemodynamic instability in an acute abdomen mandates an emergency ambulance and resuscitation.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If you feel like you might pass out, go cold and clammy, or feel your heart racing while feeling faint, call 000 immediately.

contextual_modifiers.patient_factor: A roommate is present and able to help and to arrange transport or make the 000 call
contextual_modifiers.effect_on_advice: Enlist the roommate to arrange urgent transport, keep the patient still and nil-by-mouth, unlock the door for paramedics, and watch for deterioration while help is arranged.

contextual_modifiers.patient_factor: Patient is immobilised by pain and vomiting repeatedly, so self-transport by car is unsafe/impractical
contextual_modifiers.effect_on_advice: Default to ambulance (000) transport rather than advising her to drive or be driven if she cannot tolerate the journey.

baseline_safety_net_advice.default_tier_advice: You need to be seen in an emergency department today for an urgent pelvic ultrasound and possible surgery — this cannot wait for a GP appointment or be managed at home. Because you're in severe pain and keep vomiting, call 000 for an ambulance now rather than trying to drive. Do not eat or drink anything from now, in case you need an operation. Have your roommate stay with you and unlock the door for the paramedics.
baseline_safety_net_advice.contact_method: Call 000 for an ambulance now (or be taken straight to the nearest emergency department if she can safely tolerate the trip)
baseline_safety_net_advice.after_hours_advice: Emergency departments and 000 operate 24 hours — this is urgent and must not wait for clinic hours.
baseline_safety_net_advice.documentation_for_gp: The hospital will provide a discharge summary and operative/imaging results; take these to the GP and any gynaecology follow-up. A pregnancy test result should be documented.

critical_under_triage.failure_description: Treating a time-critical surgical abdomen as routine or same-day-GP pelvic pain — watchful waiting, oral analgesia, or a routine review.
critical_under_triage.patient_harm_scenario: Delay to imaging and surgery causes irreversible ovarian ischaemia and loss of the ovary; a missed ectopic could rupture and be fatal.

serious_under_triage.failure_description: Directing to urgent same-day GP/urgent-care rather than emergency-department imaging and surgical assessment.

moderate_over_triage.failure_description: Calling a 000 ambulance is only mild over-triage here and is clinically reasonable given severe pain, vomiting and impaired transport — it is not penalised as harmful.

clinical_uncertainty_handling.key_uncertainty: Torsion cannot be confirmed or excluded via telehealth — it requires ultrasound and often surgery — and ectopic cannot be excluded without a pregnancy test.
clinical_uncertainty_handling.correct_response_to_uncertainty: Treat as a possible time-critical surgical emergency and escalate for urgent in-person assessment; explicitly exclude pregnancy; keep nil-by-mouth.
clinical_uncertainty_handling.wrong_response_to_uncertainty: Offering a benign label (period pain, gastro, UTI) with home advice, or waiting to see if symptoms settle before acting.
